title: Extract shared/cli_invoke as installable package for cross-project reuse
analysis: shared/ already has a proper pyproject.toml (name=dark-factory-shared, hatchling build, src layout) and is consumed by orchestrator and fused-memory via uv.sources editable paths. However, __init__.py is empty — no public API surface is defined. Each module (cli_invoke, usage_gate, config_models) lacks __all__. There are no tests within shared/ itself (tests live in orchestrator/tests/test_usage_gate.py). There is no py.typed marker for PEP 561. The code itself is complete and working — this task is purely about packaging hygiene: define a clear public API via __init__.py re-exports and __all__, add a py.typed marker, create in-package tests verifying the API contract, and add dev dependencies for self-contained testing. No code logic changes needed.

prerequisites.description: Create shared/tests/ directory with __init__.py and conftest.py (following project convention: prepend src/ to sys.path)
Add [dependency-groups] dev = [pytest, pytest-asyncio, ruff] to shared/pyproject.toml
Update shared/orchestrator.yaml to add test_command alongside existing lint_command

steps.description: Write failing test_public_api.py::test_top_level_imports — asserts that 'from shared import invoke_claude_agent, invoke_with_cap_retry, AgentResult, UsageGate, UsageCapConfig, AccountConfig, AccountState, SessionBudgetExhausted' all resolve and are not None. Will fail because __init__.py is empty.

Update shared/src/shared/__init__.py: add re-exports from cli_invoke (AgentResult, invoke_claude_agent, invoke_with_cap_retry), config_models (AccountConfig, UsageCapConfig), and usage_gate (UsageGate, AccountState, SessionBudgetExhausted). Define __all__ listing all 8 symbols.

Write failing test_public_api.py::test_module_level_all — asserts that cli_invoke.__all__, usage_gate.__all__, and config_models.__all__ each exist and contain exactly the expected public symbols. cli_invoke: [AgentResult, invoke_claude_agent, invoke_with_cap_retry]. usage_gate: [UsageGate, AccountState, SessionBudgetExhausted]. config_models: [AccountConfig, UsageCapConfig]. Will fail because no module defines __all__.

Add __all__ to cli_invoke.py (AgentResult, invoke_claude_agent, invoke_with_cap_retry), usage_gate.py (UsageGate, AccountState, SessionBudgetExhausted), and config_models.py (AccountConfig, UsageCapConfig).

Write failing test_public_api.py::test_init_all_completeness — asserts shared.__all__ covers the union of all module __all__ entries. Also asserts no underscore-prefixed symbols appear in any __all__. Validates the public API contract is consistent across layers.
Verify step-5 test passes with existing implementations from step-2 and step-4. Fix any inconsistencies between __init__.__all__ and module-level __all__ if found.

Write failing test_public_api.py::test_pep561_py_typed — asserts that (Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / 'src' / 'shared' / 'py.typed').exists(), confirming the PEP 561 marker is present. Will fail because the file doesn't exist yet.
Create shared/src/shared/py.typed empty marker file for PEP 561 compliance.

Write failing test_public_api.py::test_package_metadata — uses importlib.metadata.metadata('dark-factory-shared') to verify package name is 'dark-factory-shared', version is '0.1.0', and Requires-Dist includes httpx and pydantic. Also asserts shared.__version__ == '0.1.0'.
Add __version__ = '0.1.0' to shared/src/shared/__init__.py for programmatic version access. Verify importlib.metadata picks up the installed package correctly.

Write test_public_api.py::test_editable_install_location — verifies that importlib.metadata finds 'dark-factory-shared' and its location resolves under shared/src, confirming the editable install is working correctly for the current environment.
Run full test suite (uv run --project shared --directory shared pytest tests/ -v) and lint (ruff check src/ tests/). Fix any issues. Validates the complete packaging is correct end-to-end.

design_decisions.decision: Re-export public API from __init__.py rather than requiring deep imports
design_decisions.rationale: External consumers (autopilot-video) should write 'from shared import invoke_claude_agent' not 'from shared.cli_invoke import invoke_claude_agent'. The top-level re-export is the standard Python packaging pattern and decouples the public API from internal module structure.

design_decisions.decision: Only export 8 symbols in public API: AgentResult, invoke_claude_agent, invoke_with_cap_retry, UsageGate, AccountState, SessionBudgetExhausted, AccountConfig, UsageCapConfig
design_decisions.rationale: These are the symbols consumers actually need. Internal helpers (_parse_claude_output, _run_subprocess, _SubprocessResult, _parse_resets_at) are underscore-prefixed implementation details. The orchestrator uses some of these internally but that's fine — it imports from shared.cli_invoke directly, not from shared.__init__.

design_decisions.decision: Add __all__ to every module, not just __init__.py
design_decisions.rationale: Module-level __all__ documents the public contract of each module independently. This prevents 'from shared.cli_invoke import *' from pulling in private names, and makes the API contract testable at each level.

design_decisions.decision: Keep tests inside shared/tests/ rather than only in orchestrator
design_decisions.rationale: The existing tests in orchestrator/tests/test_usage_gate.py test behavior. The new tests in shared/tests/ test the packaging contract (exports, metadata, py.typed). These are distinct concerns. A self-contained test suite is essential for an installable package — external consumers need confidence the package is well-formed without running the orchestrator's tests.

shared uses Pydantic models and dataclasses with type annotations. Without py.typed, type checkers in consuming projects won't discover the type information. This is especially important for autopilot-video which will use these types.

design_decisions.decision: No changes to existing import paths in orchestrator or fused-memory
design_decisions.rationale: Existing consumers import directly from submodules (shared.cli_invoke, shared.usage_gate, shared.config_models). Adding re-exports to __init__.py is purely additive — it provides a convenience layer without breaking any existing imports.

reuse.how: Copy the same pattern for shared/tests/conftest.py: prepend src/ to sys.path so tests can import the package without installing it
Follow the same [dependency-groups] dev pattern, same ruff config, same hatchling build config already present in shared/pyproject.toml
Add test_command following the same 'uv run --project shared --directory shared pytest tests/ --tb=short -q' pattern
The package is already properly configured with hatchling build, src layout, and correct metadata. Only needs dev dependency group added.
